headline: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon to Kick Off COMPUTEX 2026 with Opening Keynote
summary: One of COMPUTEX's organizers—TAITRA (Taiwan External Trade Development Council) announced that Cristiano R. Amon, President and CEO of Qualcomm Incorporated, will deliver the Opening Keynote to kick off COMPUTEX 2026 on June 1st afternoon at the Taipei Nangang Exhibition Center Hall 2, 7F.

content.title: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon to Kick Off COMPUTEX 2026 with Opening Keynote

headline: Chipmaker Qualcomm unveils $20 billion stock buyback program
summary: Smartphone chip designer Qualcomm ‌on Tuesday unveiled ‌a $20 billion stock buyback ​program, in addition to its existing $2.1 billion share repurchase ‌authority. Shares of ⁠the company rose more than ⁠2% in premarket trading. Qualcomm is also ​increasing its ​quarterly ​cash dividend ‌by over 3%, to 92 cents per share from 89 cents, the company ‌said.

headline: Qualcomm unveils $20 billion stock buyback program
summary: Smartphone chip designer Qualcomm on Tuesday unveiled a $20 billion stock buyback ‌program as it looks to take advantage ‌of a steep drop in its share price, which has ​been hit by a global memory supply crunch that is expected to slow handset manufacturing.  Shares of the company rose more than 3% on Tuesday, after ‌a year-to-date drop ⁠of over 24% as the widespread shortage of memory chips hit Qualcomm's customers, ⁠mainly smartphone makers.  The new buyback is in addition to its existing $2.1 billion share buyback plan, the ​company said, ​adding that Qualcomm ​is also increasing its ‌quarterly cash dividend by more than 3% to 92 cents per share from 89 cents.

content.title: Qualcomm unveils $20 billion stock buyback program

Shares of the company rose more than 3% on Tuesday, after ‌a year-to-date drop ⁠of over 24% as the widespread shortage of memory chips hit Qualcomm's customers, ⁠mainly smartphone makers.  The new buyback is in addition to its existing $2.1 billion share buyback plan, the ​company said, ​adding that Qualcomm ​is also increasing its ‌quarterly cash dividend by more than 3% to 92 cents per share from 89 cents.

headline: Qualcomm Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend and Announces New $20 Billion Stock Repurchase Authorization
summary: SAN DIEGO, March 17, 2026--Qualcomm Incorporated (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved an increase in the Company’s quarterly cash dividend from $0.89 to $0.92 per share of common stock. This dividend increase will be effective for quarterly dividends payable after March 26, 2026, and will raise the annualized dividend payout to $3.68 per share of common stock.

content.title: Qualcomm Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend and Announces New $20 Billion Stock Repurchase Authorization
content.summary: SAN DIEGO, March 17, 2026--Qualcomm Incorporated (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved an increase in the Company’s quarterly cash dividend from $0.89 to $0.92 per share of common stock. This dividend increase will be effective for quarterly dividends payable after March 26, 2026, and will raise the annualized dividend payout to $3.68 per share of common stock.
